item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from here and is licensed under All Rights Reserved. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't get to the second and third pages of comments, so I don't know if anyone has said this, but I am a little disappointed that the only female personalities this thing has are defined by their interactions with men.

Along with having the shortest description by far, Donna's only quality is that she sleeps with people. Yes, it says she sometimes targets women, but there's no subtlety to her and she doesn't even get an example of her behavior like everyone else does.

Then there's Tiffany, who apparently only comes as a pair with David. Again, it's just disappointing.

I really like the idea behind what this thing is, I just think that, realistically, it would have plenty of female personalities doing the manipulating as well as male ones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was one of the first articles I read on the site that I remember really falling in love with. Coming back to it now, I think it could probably stand another round or two of clean-ups, and the cubical cell definitely shows the article's age, but dang it, I still love this. Can't believe it's been around this long and still isn't very highly rated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, since you asked so nicely…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this the beginning of le new epic period of bbaztek being active on the wiki?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I cleaned up some of the main entry so hopefully it flows a little bit better now. Thank you for the crits, brahs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As balky as the tone and writing are in places (and they are pretty awkward), this is one of my very favorite concepts on the site. I'm an introverted dude with some mild social phobias, coupled with my genuine hatred and fear of being manipulated and used by others this entry managed to scare the piss out of me. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the central conceit a lot, and so I will upvote, but it's awfully awkwardly written. The question of their being able to manifest/demanifest at will really needs addressing, for one thing.

A solution might be to say that the Foundation keeps them permanently believing they're at a party, one that just doesn't stop. As the party never ceases, they never dematerialise. Gives a sense of urgency to their containment as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I definitely really like the concept here, especially the collective's motivations, and I think overall it's executed well, but I have a few stylistic beefs with the addenda

The first addendum is inconsistent with the pronouns used for the personae - some use "it", whereas others use "him" or "her." I feel as if the former is more correct for the clinical tone, but either way I think sticking with one or the other would be less jarring. Additionally, the David persona is referred to as "Dave" exactly once, in the start of the description for Officers Brady and Barstoe, and I'm not entirely sure why.

In addition, the description of the officers' sadistic behavior, while I understand its purpose within the article, struck me as over-the-top in a negative way. It just seems… I dunno, I think especially the "record it and send it to your loved ones" detail made it seem like a scene from a horror movie going for cheap thrills.

In the second addendum, it's just that the phrase "with a formidable persuasion" strikes me as a little off? It could just be me, but I feel like "persuasiveness" would fit better.

All in all, though, I still really like the article, so I'm giving it a tentative +1 for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not too sure about this one. Felt bogged down by all the reading which I felt could have been trimmed down more. :I I don't have too much really to critique specifically, but it didn't grab me. Felt like this thing is just the random guys at parties no one invited but everyone seems ok with hanging out with until the end of the night. Dunno..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foreword: The following was recorded covertly at a fraternity party.

<Begin Log>

Shaun: Do you even lift bro? Fight me irl!

[Unknown]: Hold my forty, hold my forty—

{sound of a can of whoop-ass being opened}

<End Log>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept but the inconsistent information about its containment and capabilities is to much to overlook.

No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
._.

+1

repeattoyourselfthisisntreal.gif

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've actually decided to forgive some of this thing's flaws and give it an upvote because when you boil it down, it's a hostile, ultimately alien hive-mind entity that will expand and mimic social situations at will, and all it wants to do is hurt you, and it takes trophies. That is shitterrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you. I'm not picky about this so go nuts.

But just a friendly reminder: no rape or crazy violence. The terror of the concept stems from the power exceptional individuals can wield over others. It's not supposed to be Hostel at a keg party.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're being too harsh on yourself, this is one of my favourite ideas on the site.

… okay, the tone needs a little work and I'd love to see some more personality archetypes, too. If I think of a couple of good ones, would you be adversed to throwing them up on the page? That would be sw33t

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps the "escaping" entity can only rejoin the rest of the collective?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a hole in the plot with the David persona. If David can vanish so routinely from police lock-up, why can't 1533 similarly *poof* out of Foundation containment? This kind of goes to the confusion that Lumancer mentioned. Is 1533 an intelligence with 10 specific, physical bodies; or does it create new manifestations as needed that cease to exist when their role is played out? The article seems to indicate both at different points. For my money, bodies that appear when needed and vanish when not being directly used for interactions is creepier and I think less would have to be changed in the article as-is to make that fit.

A few more proof-reading passes are called for, particularly in the pasage about the faux-cops. "..variation of a Russian Roulette with thee nearly fully loaded chamber of a .357 S&W magnum." You play Russian Roulette, not a Russian Roulette. And it's pretty standard to use the whole gun, because a bullet won't fire if you just use the chamber by itself.

Last nitpick: giving it anything to learn from, even socially maladjusted individuals, would be a very bad idea. Unless 1533 is constantly forgetting past tricks and only mimics its most recent social observations, which wasn't the impression I got, then "feeding" it autistics won't make it any less socially adept. Yes, it probably won't learn much from Rain-man, but it might learn something. And every little bit it does glean, added to what it already knows, makes it that much more effective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I totally understand that feeling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had the same question about the manifesting-vs-physical existence thing. I think it would work either way, though obviously it'd have to be rewritten slightly.

I dunno. I really love the idea of a malevolent party entity, slowly learning how to mimic human behavior. I wish there was at least a hint of its origins—is it an artificial creation? Maybe a naturally evolved predator? Either way, it's earned my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeesh, tough crowd. Nah, I'm joshing. I worked too long on this idea and I guess I got burned out. I was inspired by a lot of things, the movie Kids, the Prince of Nothing series, lots of stuff, and it didn't mesh so well. I'll address the issues that were brought up and let this sink. Fuck a rewrite. I can't look at this shit anymore. I mean, two Davids? jesus christ

As for your points Lumancer, I definitely considered them but the article was bloated as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone already mentioned, you have two Davids. The tone could definitely use some shoring up, but this is such a long article that identifying all of the offending lines is going to take some time.

Above all else, something about the concept doesn't add up to me. Early on in the description you state that the entities manifest only during the party events, but given that they haven't vanished from containment and that they were organizing the parties prior to that, this doesn't seem to be accurate. However, if they DO exist outside the parties, then what the hell did they do with their time before they were caught?

Also, why did the Foundation let them keep throwing parties in the wild as long as they did before taking them in? The fact that things had to get as bad as they did during the police scenario seems like a hell of a dropped ball.

I think you're on to a very solid idea here, but the current execution leaves a lot to be desired for clarity, continuity, and conciseness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad.

Edit: But now that I think about it, not quite good enough for me to upvote. Awkward…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Execution needs too much work for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sociopathic, or fascistic, perhaps, but erroneous does give a funny tone to it. "We know his assertions are false because we've successfully contained Love at Site-34."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The entity has repeatedly expressed erroneous views on the inherent inferiority of women, the right of powerful persons to subjugate the weak, the existential loneliness of human life, and the impossibility of love in a deterministic universe, but in an eloquent and authoritative manner."

I'm not sure that the Foundation would comment on whether or not his views are "right" or "wrong", I think you should call them something that refers to their unorthodoxy rather than their "erroneousness".

Also, there are two of them named "David". Not sure if that's intentional.

That said, I dig the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fixed, thanks tl. definitely gonna tone down the wordiness for my next one. this was an idea I absolutely needed to put down in all its wordiness though, it was like a weird writing itch that wouldn't go away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the idea, though the article is a bit wordy and tough to follow in spots. Not sure the addendum really adds anything, and you also mean "foment", not "ferment".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fixed, thank you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Each entity receives a specific personality schemata

"Schemata" is plural, "schema" singular

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be a bit lolfoundation, but putting these people into the scripted nightclub would be an amazing story if anyone knows a good hook for it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's fair. I'll tone it down a bit.

e: there, the quoted sections are toned down a bit. thanks for taking the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically all of it. "Purple" might not be quite the right word. Perhaps "overblown"? Some samples:

personnel discovered smuggling paraphernalia into SCP-1533's holding cell will be reprimanded to the fullest extent of Site-██ disciplinary protocol

The entities that comprise SCP-1533 are capable of altering their personalities by flawlessly mimicking the actions and temperaments of human subjects they have interacted with in the past.

remaining acutely sensitive to every nuance in human intercourse even in when in an apparently inebriated state

an entity conferring with an eloquent individual concerned about human rights will only assimilate the person's diction and not necessarily his/her sensibilities.

The ease by which SCP-1533 is able to form discrete, seemingly well-adjusted personalities from a menagerie of human behaviors is a testament to the intelligence's social savviness.

to becoming a highly alluring, adaptive, and dangerous group of individuals

exists only to manipulate human beings to inflict torment on themselves or each other

The collective takes a particular pleasure in employing charismatic personalities to captivate human targets

The entities are unique in having attempted to bribe operatives not with material goods but with offers of an unrequited love's adoration,

entity

entities

entities

entities

entity

Honestly, I could go on. I'm sorry, as I said, the concept isn't bad, but this writing is just kind of bad PSA.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you show me where the writing is purple if you don't mind?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This writing is far, far too purple. I can't take this seriously. It's also kind of unclear how the Foundation got all this information. Were they just letting these things operate freely?

The concept isn't bad, it's just that I don't feel any of the concept, the writing, and the SCP format work too well together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough. Anyway, this is pretty awesome. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wanted to go for something that can distill the most charismatic and endearing attributes of every person it meets into a few personalities. Something that's as mindless and impersonal as a force of nature: it just does what it does. An entity that can speak to your soul, but gets its jollies off playing you like a fiddle is pretty fucking scary to me.

e: if you want a goal, you can imagine the entities want to escape and assimilate so much data they become modern-day jesus figures and take the entire planet by storm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. I especially love the strategic attempts to retard its social functioning.

While this is probably fine as is, I think I would like it even better if the entities had some sort of goal in mind with their harassment of people, beyond the "because it's fun" angle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh my god its an EVIL PARTY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an extremely clever idea. One tiny nitpick is that the word "savviness" hurts the tone. +1
